title: Limited companies
summary: 9706 P2 — share capital, reserves, and basic company financial statements.
learningObjectives: Distinguish ordinary and preference share capital.
Explain share premium and retained earnings.
Prepare basic company SPL and SOFP.
Outline dividends and their accounting treatment.

simpleExplanation.title: Company accounts basics
simpleExplanation.summary: Limited companies show equity as share capital plus reserves — profit after tax increases retained earnings; dividends reduce them.
steps: Share capital — nominal value of shares issued.
Share premium — excess over nominal on issue.

flashcards.back: Variable dividend; voting rights; residual claim.
Credit balance — amount above nominal on share issue.
Disclose in notes; not a liability until approved/paid (syllabus treatment).
Shareholders lose at most amount invested.

sections.content: **Limited companies** (1.5.4) completes Section 1.5. Know **equity section layout**: share capital, share premium, retained earnings.

sections.question: Company issued 200,000 ordinary shares of $0.50 at $1.20 each. Retained earnings opening $40,000. Profit for year $55,000. Dividend paid $15,000. Show equity section extracts.

sections.label: Mark a limited company accounts question
